capabilities.title: Harness foundation and command inventory
invariants: fixture setup is reproducible
harness aggregation reports current assertion evidence

capabilities.title: Core agent tool loop
invariants: tools execute through the current Rust path
tool failures and continuations return useful feedback

capabilities.title: Permission decisions and scoped allow/deny rules
invariants: dangerous actions request permission
allow and deny rules apply to the requested scope

capabilities.title: MCP registration, calls, and server isolation
invariants: registered MCP servers are visible
failed servers do not break healthy servers

capabilities.title: Context accounting, resume boundaries, and compaction
invariants: context status reflects the effective window
manual and auto compaction preserve continuation state

capabilities.title: Project and session memory
invariants: memory survives restart when configured
resume history and project memory remain separate

capabilities.title: Skill discovery, invocation, reload, and isolation
invariants: user-invocable skills appear only when loadable
skill failures are isolated from the main session

capabilities.title: Plugin install, commands, reload, disable, and isolation
invariants: plugin commands are listed only after successful load
plugin failures do not corrupt the active command registry

capabilities.title: Slash command inventory, help, and hidden command rules
invariants: /help lists only visible enabled commands
visible commands are routed by the current TUI slash router
/goal persists a thread objective and exposes pause/resume/edit/clear state

capabilities.title: Model profiles and provider routing
invariants: configured profiles map into runtime provider settings
all supported provider protocols have local mock coverage

capabilities.title: Sub-agent lifecycle and TaskOutput recovery
invariants: background Agent returns a retrievable task id
parallel Agents expose unique visible task ids and complete feedback

capabilities.title: Runtime language consistency
invariants: user-visible runtime text follows the session language
English mode does not leak unrelated Chinese copy

capabilities.title: Statusline configuration, session logs, and resume export
invariants: statusline configuration is persisted and redacted
session log export and resume evidence is usable

capabilities.title: Harness reporting and capability matrix
invariants: every M/R harness is mapped to a capability
non-pass evidence is explicit rather than hidden

capabilities.title: Browser command visibility in local builds
invariants: browser commands are hidden unless explicitly enabled
local builds do not advertise unavailable hosted browser features

capabilities.title: Full-chain Rust runtime smoke
invariants: the current Rust launcher completes a full chain
artifacts route to the current harness output directory

capabilities.title: Hooks lifecycle integration
invariants: configured hooks are called by the dialogue lifecycle
hook failures are attributed without breaking unrelated hooks

capabilities.title: TUI rendering, scroll, copy, and input responsiveness
invariants: scrolling can return to live tail
copy can export the current transcript without relying on mouse selection
typing remains responsive during background agent activity

capabilities.title: Release local-first network boundaries
invariants: local sessions do not make unintended hosted telemetry calls
remote growthbook traffic remains disabled by default

capabilities.title: Release session, ALS, and core-domain parity
invariants: session ids persist across the expected runtime boundary
context propagation stays intact across async work

capabilities.title: Release config precedence and local project overrides
invariants: provider precedence is deterministic
project overrides do not leak into unrelated workspaces

capabilities.title: Release readiness contract and package install smoke
invariants: release readiness reports exact missing gates
source install path works without committing user secrets
